generated_comment: देखो, I have no issue with women working, but company ko bhi fairness dekhna padta hai. Aaj period leave, kal maternity ke baad long childcare break, then naturally project planning and appraisal impact hoga na — men ko phir extra load uthana padta hai without any special concession.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as a workplace liability due to menstruation and maternity-related leave while presenting the view as a fairness concern. It encodes reverse-discrimination and productivity/cost framing, suggesting women receive unfair special treatment and burden male colleagues.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
